Lactantius quote the several description the heathen philosopher make of God , Lib. 1. cap . 5. among the rest , quote Chrysippus describe he to be , naturalem vim divinâ ration praeditam . a natural power endue with divine reason . for they evident see a divine wisdom join with power in all the work of creation . Argum. 3. we see in the creation the excellent proportion and usefulness of one part to another for the preservation of the whole , and the suitableness of the whole to man that be make the lord of it . the life of man or beast can be preserve without food ; food they can have unless the earth bring it forth ; bring it forth it can unless it be water with shower from heaven ; shower there can be without cloud ; cloud can be without vapour ; vapour there can be , unless they be exhale by the sun from the water ; and for this end the sunbeam do not only bring down light and heat to the earth , but attract vapour from it towards heaven , and all for the service of the earth . wherein we see the heaven hear the earth , and the earth hear the corn , wine , and oil , and other fruit , and all hear man and as in this low world one part of the creation be support by another , as the earth by the water and air , so this whole low world be preserve by the superior world , and under the dominion of it , which show the excellent order of thing in nature ; each part tend to preserve the whole , which the philosopher call the harmony of the universe . as one of they speak , natura est perfectissima Harmonia ; there be no harmony better than the order of nature . for as in harmony , though there be several sound , yet be they so temper and modulate , as to make pleasant music . so the several part of the universe be so adapt and fit to each other , as that they all do constitute a harmonious order . and as harmony arise out of discord , so there be in the universe , Concordia discourse , creature of contrary nature and quality , yet so mix and proportion , as that they sweet agree together , and harmonise with each other , and serve mutual to the preservation of one another . and if we consider how the creation be fit to the use of man , and man be fit to it , who be make the lord of it , we may easy discern this be do by some wise agent . as there be many pleasant quality in natural thing , so man have several bod•ly sense , whereby to discern and enjoy the pleasantness of they . to what purpose be there sweet smell , beautiful colour , pleasant taste in the flower and fruit of the earth , if there be no such sense , as see , taste , or smell . and so to what purpose will these sense be in the body of man , if there be not suitable object provide in nature for they . and so when we observe in these fruit of the earth such virtue and quality that be both nourish and medicinal to the body of man , and some of they have signature and character upon they that declare their virtue , and what part of man body they be useful to , it show they be create for the use of man as we see a man that build a house for his use , he do fit it with room , furniture , and utensil , to make it a convenient habitation . So have God fit this world to be a habitation for man , who be make to be the inhabitant of it , act 17. 26. and as the nature of man do affect variety , so have God furnish the world with infinite variety of creature , for the gratify of his sense , and for the delightful exercise of his mind ; and by all set forth his own manifold wisdom ; and commend himself to man by his great goodness and bounty herein . and there be such a suitableness betwixt the world and man , that man be call a little world . but the great world do serve the lesser . after God have make and finish the rest of his work , he then make man , and epitomize they all in he ; and give he dominion over they : and the exact fitness of the several part of the creation to his use , do show that it be the effect of some infinite wisdom . Arg. 4. whence be it that those two natural principle of self-preservation , and self-propagation , be so inviolable found in the nature of all live creature ; even those that have no reason , as well as those that have : both which be necessary to the preservation of the universe . may not we hence easy argue , that sure this be do intentional for such a end . and if intentional , then it be do by reason ; and if by reason , it must be by his reason that first make this universe . for be it not rational , that when the creature be make they shall be preserve , both in the individual and the several species or kind of their be ; and what can do this better in a natural course , than for they to have these two principle inherent in their several nature . and so we may observe in vegetable , as herb , flower , tree ; they all have a seed in themselves , which nature bring forth for the preservation of their several kind in a continue succession of individual of the same kind . and whence be this rational provision in nature , but by some rational agent , and who can that be but the god of nature , that be before it , and found this rational law in it , and be the natura naturans , as philosopher speak : that nature that be the original and lawgiver of nature . Arg. 5. we may observe in the creation there be degree of be , and this show there be some chief be . and there be degree of perfection , which show there be some chief perfection . for else we can not compute degree , if there be no first , with respect to which such computation be to be make . these degree in nature be by learned man call the scale of nature ; and we must come to some top in the scale or ladder , and not ascend in infinitum , though we must into infinity , which be the infinite God . for , as i say before , where there be prius and posterius , there be primum and postremum ; so where there be magi and minus , there be maximum and minimum ; where there be degree of perfection , there must needs be some great perfection , and what can that be , but that God who be optimus and maximus , who be the most excellent be , and the first perfection . for instance ; we see some knowledge in brute , but more in man , more in angel , but there be a be then that be perfect in knowledge . and so i may speak of all perfection , whether natural or moral ; the degree of they show there be one first . or , if you will look upon these degree of be among the creature as several part of be , we may again argue , where there be part , there must be some whole ; and where there be particular , there must be some universal . now we see the creature be be but a particular be , and in their several kind we see several part of be , none of they have all be in themselves , and therefore there must be some totum of be , or some be that be universal , that comprehend in it self all be , and what can this be but God alone . Argum. 6. we see in the creation how creature that be without reason , yet act accord to the exact rule of reason ; which can not be , be they not guide and act by some other principle than their own respective nature . for no nature can act of it self beyond its own principle : as we see how the be do make its comb , and the spider its web , and the bird their nest , and beaver their house in the water , consist of several story ( as i have hear from eye-witness ) accord to the exact rule of art and reason . and if you say , that these creature have reason quoad hoc , as to those thing that belong to the benefit of their own nature , though not to other thing ; yet what shall we say to those creature that have not so much as sense , or a sensitive life ; as plant and tree in this low world . whence be it that the ivy , as if it do know it be a weak plant , and can not subsist of itself shall natural bend to the wall or tree that be near it , there to fix , and as it be incorporate itself by a strange and firm in . hesion ? whence be it that a tree do fix its root with that strength and art in the bowel of the earth , that no man can imagine how it can be do better , as if it do know what blast of wind it must encounter with , which threaten its subversion , etc and what wisdom be that which guide and carry the root downward , when the bough and branch ascend upward ; as if it understand the need it stand in of the fatness and moisture of the earth , and therefore do send forth its root up and down through the earth to forage , and fetch it in . so also , if we consider how curious the embryo or infant be form in the womb of she that be with child ; the formative virtue that do this be ignorant of what it do , and yet every part be form to a rational end in nature . and whence be it in the upper world that the sun be place at such a convenient distance from the earth , and steer its motion by such a line as be most for the benefit of it . that when it be go to its Tropic in the south , it shall return back again to the north , as if it do rational provide for the benefit of each part and region of the low world ? in so much that the psalmist do attribute knowledge to it , Psal. 104. 19 the sun know its go down . and as Tully argue concern the heaven , their motion be either by nature , chance , or prudence . not by nature , for if they be light , they will move upward ; if heavy , they will move downward ; if neither , they will stand still : nor by chance , for they move in a constant and regular order , where there be nothing temerarium nec fortuitum , nothing rash nor accidental , as he speak Lib. 2. de Nat. Argum. 7. we see this order in nature , that all creature do serve some end above themselves ; as creature without life do serve those creature that have life , and those creature that have only a vegetative life serve the creature that have a sensitive life , and those that have a sensitive life do serve man that have a superior rational life ; and therefore man also be make to serve some end above himself : and what can this be but to serve his creator . for there be no end that man can serve above himself , but serve God , which may evidence to we that God be . and as there be some equity and reason that those thing in nature which be inferior shall serve the superior , so it be the high reason in the world , that man shall serve he that be infinite superior to he , and supreme over all , and which be that end above man which he be make to serve . Argum. 8. last , we see all thing in nature under certain bound and limit . as the earth , the water , the sun , the moon , the star , the heaven ; so be all live creature bound and limit in their several nature . and within these bound they still abide . now how come all thing to be thus circumscribe and limit ? why be their several nature extend thus far and no far , when it be natural to the creature to be extensive of themselves towards some further perfection in their being ? natural body be under limit , and so be create spirit , as angel in their several order or denomination , and the soul of man . now who have set these limit , but that first be , who be unlimited , and have make all thing in number , weight and measure ? it be he that have give the earth its dimension , and confine the water within their bank , that comprehend the dust of the earth in a measure ; that weigh the hill in scale , and the mountain in a balance . it be he that have mete out the heaven with his span , and appoint the number of the star , and call they all by name . which the grecian mean by call their chief god Jupiter , nonlatinalphabet , or the limiter ; and the Romans call he Jovem Terminalem . as God have give the creature their being , so he have set they the several bound of their be . and to make this yet more plain in a few instance . whence be it that a stone have not a vegetative life as well as a tree ; and a tree a sensitive life as well as a beast ; and a beast reason as well as a man ; and why be not man intellectual spirit as well as angel ? all creature be bound in their several nature , and what , or who can thus bind they , but the god and author of nature . so that upon the whole i may conclude , that if the world be a live animal , as Plato fancy , and know it self , it will ready acknowledge its begin and be from God alone .
